_comment: skeleton.config.json — source of truth for all app configuration. The dev shell agent reads this at session start and writes to it on save. v1.3: renamed from skeleton.config.json. The 'cactai_' prefix is reserved for platform-side tables and identifiers; customer apps use the neutral 'skeleton' prefix.

_origin._comment: The auth-framework version this app was provisioned with. Sprint 6 populates this from @cactai-io/auth-framework-reference/FRAMEWORK_VERSION.txt. Used by the platform to detect when a customer app is running on an older framework than is currently shipped.

tenancy._comment_model: v1.3 — multi_user_multi_tenant | single_user_multi_tenant | multi_user_single_tenant. Set during workflow Stage 2. Pre-v1.3 values 'single' and 'multi' are no longer valid.

signup._comment_style: v1.3 — open | invite_only | gated. open = anyone with a valid email can sign up; invite_only = signup requires an invitation token from an existing tenant member; gated = signup goes through a manual approval queue. Set during workflow Stage 4.

collaboration._comment_enabled: v1.3 — When true, this app exposes cross-tenant collaboration features: a user in tenant A can be invited to act in tenant B (Option X tenant-to-tenant) or be added directly as a user on a tenant B resource (Option Z direct human). When false, tenants are strictly isolated.

provider._comment_model: anthropic | openai — set during workflow setup
provider._comment_mode: included | byok — included means Cactai provides the key, byok means developer provides it
provider._comment_offer_choice: When true, end users can select their preferred provider from the ones the developer has configured

auth._comment_providers: Array of enabled providers: google | apple | email

roles._comment: v1.3 — Role keys named freely by the developer (not constrained to super_admin/admin/user). Each role's value is the list of capability strings cumulatively granted. Capabilities are checked via current_active_role_has_capability() on the customer DB.

decisions._comment: Workflow decisions recorded here as source of truth.

skills._comment: Active skill registrations. Key is artifact_type, value is skill_id.

protocols._comment: Workflow protocols this app supports. Key is protocol_id, value is protocol definition. Written by the agent during the workflow.

schema._comment: Data entity descriptions. SQL migrations live in /config/schema/.

media._comment_image_domains: Allowed external image domains for next/image.

sprints._comment: Sprint metadata written by the agent during the workflow.
